inline_review:
commit c59509536dcca61cec1bf2f3035c1acd03751cf7
Author: Lorenzo Stoakes (Oracle) <ljs@kernel.org>
mm/zswap: add missing kunmap_local()

This commit fixes a kmap slot leak in the uncompressed page path of
zswap_decompress() introduced by a previous commit. It explicitly unmaps
the locally mapped folio.

Closes: https://lore.kernel.org/all/69b75e2c.050a0220.12d28.015a.GAE@google.com

> diff --git a/mm/zswap.c b/mm/zswap.c
> index e6ec3295bdb0..499520f65ff0 100644
> --- a/mm/zswap.c
> +++ b/mm/zswap.c
> @@ -942,9 +942,14 @@ static bool zswap_decompress(struct zswap_entry *entry, struct folio *folio)
>  
>  	/* zswap entries of length PAGE_SIZE are not compressed. */
>  	if (entry->length == PAGE_SIZE) {
> +		void *dst;
> +
>  		WARN_ON_ONCE(input->length != PAGE_SIZE);
> -		memcpy_from_sglist(kmap_local_folio(folio, 0), input, 0, PAGE_SIZE);
> +
> +		dst = kmap_local_folio(folio, 0);
> +		memcpy_from_sglist(dst, input, 0, PAGE_SIZE);
>  		dlen = PAGE_SIZE;
> +		kunmap_local(dst);

Does this code also need a flush_dcache_folio() call?

Commit e2c3b6b21c77 replaced memcpy_to_folio() with memcpy_from_sglist()
and kmap_local_folio() here. The original memcpy_to_folio() implementation
internally invoked flush_dcache_folio() after the copy.

Because memcpy_from_sglist() only reads from the SG list to a raw pointer and
does not flush the data cache of the destination buffer, does this leave the
D-cache unflushed?

On architectures with VIVT or aliasing VIPT caches, could this cause
applications to read stale data when the folio is later mapped to user space?

>  	} else {
>  		sg_init_table(&output, 1);
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"The patch fails to restore a required data cache flush that was dropped in the commit it fixes, leading to potential data corruption on architectures with aliasing data caches.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"1. Commit e2c3b6b21c77 replaced `memcpy_to_folio()` with `memcpy_from_sglist()` and an open-coded `kmap_local_folio()` in the uncompressed data path (`entry->length == PAGE_SIZE`).
2. The previous implementation used `memcpy_to_folio()`, which correctly invoked `flush_dcache_folio(folio)` internally after the copy.
3. `memcpy_from_sglist()` reads from the SG list to a raw pointer and does not flush the data cache of the destination buffer. (The crypto API path safely relies on `scatterwalk_done_dst()` to flush the destination).
4. The current patch adds `kunmap_local()` but still misses the `flush_dcache_folio(folio)` call. Without this explicit flush, the kernel's writes via the kmap virtual address might remain in the CPU's D-cache. When the folio is subsequently mapped into user space, applications on architectures with VIVT or aliasing VIPT caches may read stale data, leading to memory corruption."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit c59509536dcca61cec1bf2f3035c1acd03751cf7

> +		memcpy_from_sglist(dst, input, 0, PAGE_SIZE);
>  		dlen = PAGE_SIZE;
> +		kunmap_local(dst);
